title: firstline: truncate in place instead of re-alloc
description: In apply_set_modifiers (evaluator.rs), the :firstline branch does v[..end].to_string() to shorten an owned String. This forces a new heap allocation and memcpy, then drops the original buffer. The idiomatic approach for an owned String is v.truncate(end); v — which shortens the existing buffer in place with no allocation. Change: replace 'v[..end].to_string()' with 'v.truncate(end); v'.
